reviews.review_text: We are in our own caravan
Can't fault it.  Staff helpful,  very clean park,  large caravan sites on the beach and across the road from great restaurants and famous kingscliffe beach hotel.

This is by far the nicest park we have ever stayed at.  The park is extremely clean and well set out.  The camp kitchem has a co-working desk for those working remotely which is a great addition to park, that I had not seen before.  The beach is directly in front of the park.  The street behind the parks has a diverse range of eateries and restauants, Woolworths is a 5 minute walk. Sites are large and easy to get in to.  Staff and friendly.  All sites are double slabs.  We have eaten a the Bowling Club and Surf Club which are beach side, on either side of the park, and would recommend both.  You do need to book at the Surf Club. There is a great beach side walk that takes you to the breakwall.  Im not sure of the site number but it is attached to ensuite number 4.  If I had to find an area for improvment, it would only be that the ensuites dont have a vanity, so you need to put your toiletries on a small shelf.  If we booked again we would try and get a beachfront site, but really all the sites in this park are great.

This is by far the nicest park we have ever stayed at.  The park is extremely clean and well set out.  The camp kitchem has a co-working desk for those working remotely which is a great addition to park, that I had not seen before.  The beach is directly in front of the park.  The street behind the parks has a diverse range of eateries and restauants, Woolworths is a 5 minute walk. Sites are large and easy to get in to.  Staff and friendly.  All sites are double slabs.  We have eaten a the Bowling Club and Surf Club which are beach side, on either side of the park, and would recommend both.  You do need to book at the Surf Club. There is a great beach side walk that takes you to the breakwall.  Im not sure of the site number but it is attached to ensuite number 4.  If I had to find an area for improvment, it would only be that the ensuites dont have a vanity, so you need to put your toiletries on a small shelf.  If we booked again we would try and get a beachfront site, but really all the sites in this park are great.
Great location on Kingscliff Beach across from main street. Supermarket, pub, takeaway and restaurants all an easy walk.  Excellent, well looked after park and amenities with friendly staff.

It'sone of the most beautiful units we've  ever stayed in :-) and being able to walk straight onto the beach was amazing
Staff were all warm & friendly
Great park but very expensive.

We stayed 4 nights during the September school holidays in a beachfront site. The park is well maintained, facilities are kept clean, and it’s in a great location close to the beach and right in town. The onsite managers are friendly.

Unfortunately we and several fellow campers staying around the perimeter of the park had trouble with a group of local teens during our stay. We had things stolen from our campsites, were verbally abused, and fire crackers/water balloons/full cans of soft drinks thrown at campsites during the afternoon and well into the night.

The problem with this park is there is only a four to five foot fence around the perimeter. That’s lovely to enjoy the ocean view, but if you are staying on a beachfront site or a site that’s on the perimeter, you are sitting ducks for the local youth crime. On the perimeter sites you have the main road and pub on the western side, a local park on the southern side, and a public pathway then sand dune on the eastern side. There is ample opportunity for outsiders to jump the small fence, steal belongings, and retreat to the seclusion of the public park or behind the sand dune.

There is also no lock on the gate on the eastern perimeter. Anybody can come in and out, and we witnessed the same group of teens enter the park during the day unchallenged. There are also no security cameras on the eastern and southern perimeters.

After speaking to fellow campers who have stayed here in the past, and one of the receptionists, theft is a repeating occurrence. It is a real shame as this is a lovely park and Kingscliff is a beautiful little town. Like anywhere these days there is youth crime, which is of no fault of the park management. After speaking with the onsite manager, he was extremely helpful and apologetic, and gave us his personal mobile number to call if we had further incidents. We were really happy with his response.

We felt compelled to write this review to warn fellow campers to be vigilant and lock up when staying here. I would also strongly suggest not booking a beachfront site, they’re more expensive and honestly you’re a sitting duck to any trouble, and you’ve got zero privacy due to the busy public pathway right behind your site.

Again this is a lovely park and we were grateful for the response of the onsite manager. Apart from these incidents we had a great stay and the kids had an absolute ball.

Stayed on a ensuite site for seven nights, friendly greeting upon check-in, superb location easy walk to all amenities, lots of coffee shops, cafes and Tavern/bowling club right next door
Camp kitchen is Ultra modern but lacks equipment (ie/ nothing )  no cloth or tools to ensure guests leave the kitchen clean. Bones that has an oven the electric barbecues are the hottest we have ever used.
This is an immaculate park , touch on the expensive side but that seems to be the trend these days
Compliments to the team
Great van Park.

We recently stayed in our own van on a powered site. Lovely park,great communication, very clean ammenities and generous sized sites. Fantastic location, next to beach with bowls club, surf club, restaurant, cafes and shops, all in walking distance. Can get some noise from the tavern late at night. Only negative is that we couldnt take our dog with us!

Our experience was clouded by the fact that our bikes which were locked were stolen at 4.20am  while we stayed at this holiday park. The park is totally insecure. Our site was beach front which cost more than the other sites. There is no security surveillance along the beach front area where a public walkway is located along with a low fence and unlocked gates.. The staff acknowledged that they were concerned about security in that area of the park but still no cameras have been placed there. We moved into the centre of the park for our second night there. Proximity to shops is good but we won’t be back. It would have been a nice gesture from park staff to get a partial refund as the beach front site cost more but is very high risk.
Recently Re-built. Large powered sites facing towards the beach. Clean modern amenities. Could do with some trees throughout the park as it does look a bit bare.
This place is 5 star.. Staff were professional and helpful, camp kitchen is beautiful and clean, modern...location is absolutely perfect...Beach with crystal clear water...definitely staying again

The park itself is great. Facilities are spotless and there is a good variety of shops, restaurants and cafes close by.|Major let down was the noise from the Main Street - excessive loud music noise late Saturday night. Absolutely ridiculous. Terrible. Will not be back.

Love the location, proximity to beach, great restaurants & shopping as well as the vide.  ABSOLUTELY HATE that local Council & park management has decided to fleece future battlers and families by doubling the nightly rate.  Un Australian!

Great park. The amenities were so clean shout out to the lovely male cleaner that went above and  beyond. Didn’t  get his name but great work to you hope this gets to you.
Beautiful park great atmosphere facilities and close to everything. And love the fake grass areas.

$131 for a powered caravan site on the beach for 2 persons in non school holiday time!!! Are you kidding me!! What is it that you are providing for me when I have my own caravan??This is way overpriced. In fact in 31 years of caravanning this outrageous cost takes the prize. 🏆
Love staying here can’t wait to return

Dayna is very welcoming, friendly and full of recommendations for all sorts of things. She warned me that the rates were pricey this week, however I thought it was fair given the prime location, immaculate facilities and helpful communication via texts.
Loved my stay here and would recommend to anyone

We stayed in an ensuite powered site. Great location right on the beach and across the road from shops and pub. Sites are generous and right next door to the bowling club. Would definitely recommend this park.

We stopped here for 2 nights with 4 kids. The staff at reception were very welcoming. The caravan spots are generously sized and easy to navigate into. The park is right on next to the beach with amazing views. All in all a 10/10 would highly recommend 👌

Love this place. Great staff and excellent location to everywhere you want within walking distance in Kingscliff. Ammenities are super clean. Toilets and showers are spotless all the time. Community kitchen area is superb. Just be careful when going to the beach though. Signs on beach say beware snakes. We saw two brown snakes out as early as before 10 am on an August morning. All up a fantastic site,would stay here again no problem. Well worth booking into.
Great location, staff friendly and helpful, well maintained c/park, sites big enough for your van and car ,shops directly across the road, club next door,

My wife and I stayed at Tweed Holiday Parks Kingscliff Beach in our caravan on our winter escape trip.  We had an ensuite site on a corner, so we had plenty of room.  The ensuite was good, but it was in need of a tidy up as it was showing some age.  The park itself is really good with plenty of space and good roads.  It looked to us that site access for RVs is no problem.|We really love the location of the park as it is across the road from a multitude of shops and a great pub.  There is also a small shopping centre a very easy walk away, which includes a major supermarket.|Best of all, there is a Bowling Club next door, we loved it so much I became a member.  The food is good and good value.  There is also a Surf Club at the opposite end, but we did not go there.|The big attraction of course is the beach, which is no more than 5-m from anywhere in the park.  In good weather this is a fantastic beach, unfortunately it rained a lot when we were there!  But this did not ruin our stay as there is so many other things to do.||Two important things for many RV travellers,  there is good mobile and good TV reception!|We will definitely return - already booked!||Overall, we give Tweed Holiday Parks Kingscliff Beach 5 stars out of 5.

It's a beautiful caravan park but the people are completely unreasonable.  I booked for 6 days and when I got the confirmation it stated it was for 7 days.  I immediately went back to them to say there was an error and they said it couldn't be them and charged me $40 to change the booking.  Next level arrogance and the customer is definitely not always right with this business.  After years of staying there it's time for a change.

Nice clean basic caravan park in main street, friendly staff, lovely amenities. But be aware, because it's in the main street it is noisy. 21:30 we had a party with extremely loud music coming from the bar across the road and traffic early in the morning. May be better if you are lucky enough to get a site beach side. Still had a nice stay though.
Well Located near shops but the park itself doest have a tree anywhere. It's a bit of a concrete jungle. Beach is right out the back so that's good.
Always lovely ! Spotlessly clean, maintained sites! Best camp kitchen ever !!!

Absolutely beautiful site in a fantastic location.
Lady on check in was super helpful and friendly and the updates by text about markets and other things to look out for are great.
No wonder it's booked out all the time.
Will definitely be back when we can get a spot haha
Such a wonderful place
The cabin was excellent and the location was good for us. Will definitely visit again 😀

Great stay at Tweed Holiday Parks Kingscliff North. The cabin was clean and comfortable, and the grounds and facilities were excellent. Easy access to the beach was a big plus. Only downside is that you need to drive to reach the Kingscliff township. Although makes for a good bike ride.
Great little park, neat and clean. Was a bit tight for our rig but ok. Nice beach.
3night stop over to visit relatives A1 class
Expensive now as the council have gotten greedy. But the staff and managers there are lovely as always. The beach is nice and over all i love coming here
If you like a soft mattress that sinks in you'll be fine. My back hurts.

This is a private block of units NOT a holiday park!
I wish Google would fix this issue!
Please contact Google to get our building delisted.  We’ve tried many times without success.
Great place to stay. Quiet, clean and right on the beach
Stayed in the cabins. Was absolutely perfect. Clean, new and great location. Cabins are across from the beach .

What a beautiful, clean and well appointed holiday village. We stayed in Kingfisher 5 cabin that had ocean views where we sat and watched the whales each morning having breakfast. The staff were really helpful and friendly and the beaches were fabulous to walk on as they were flat and went forever. |The walk to Fingal Head took 1hr 20mins from village. Lovely little place to explore. |Kingscliff itself is a short walk to shops, pubs and groceries either on the sand or via footpaths.
Always be good

We stayed in a Sea Breeze cabin. Great location to the beach and great views. The room was very clean and has everything needed with plenty of room. The staff was very friendly and very helpful. We enjoyed our stay very much and would stay again!

We've stayed here a few times now and love it (in the cabins as well as camping). The location is great and central to anything around the Tweed Coast area. The staff are also great - friendly, helpful and very accommodating. The park itself is clean and has great views of the beach. When we camp, the artificial grass is a big winner for us - it helps keep things clean, and means you don't have dust, sand, etc, blowing into your site. Overall, it's a great park and we highly recommend it.
Wow, what a great caravan park. Sites were level, and access was easy. Everything was spotlessly clean. Location is great.

Amazing location on the beach at Kingscliff north end - quieter than the main hub. Our Kingfisher cabin looked out onto the beach and made for excellent views for morning coffees and evening beers! Great for families! The cabins are near new and suited us great with a 3 year old with us too!
We will be back - it's our new favourite mini getaway spot!
Rang 10 van parks. None answered or rang back except Kim at Reflections Kingscliff. Professional & helpful plus had a site available!

Sadly we only had one night here. |It is an immaculate clean park . Katherine , at the front desk gave us a fabulous spot overlooking the beach. |This park is a 15min walk ( depending on how fast you walk!!) to kingscliff village. |Thanks Katherine for looking after us so well. We will see you again!
Great park at a great price right on the beach. The staff are friendly and knowledgeable about the area. It's a short walk to the pub and shops.

Would have given 5 stars except for security issues with the access doors to our cabin.  Unable lock the glass sliding door or the screen door.  They botched the fix for the glass door, which still took some fiddling to lock.  The screen door was never fixed.  There was a theft on the Wednesday night, but we had triple locked our bikes and set up barriers for the doors.  Overall the cabin was excellent, clean and spacious.  A nice walk to the restaurants, and the bike paths were great, except for the teenagers on electric motor cycles breaking all path rules and generally being reckless.  Not much beach, thanks to the cyclone, but still a nice place to walk.

Outstanding cabin, what a lovely surprise
We stayed in  Seaview 2 , older style but totally renovated inside
Balcony is huge

It’s Better than an appartment and so quiet
Very modern and tastefully decorated
Beautiful beach views
Staff were so friendly , helpful and professional

Will definitely return next year

Our group stayed in Dreamtime cabins 2 and 3. The cabins were presented quite well and were clean but the there was no view of the beach from either cabin. My main problem was with the beds, the main beds in both cabins had totally collapsed on both sides, I’d hate to think how old they were, given that my bed at home is 10 years old and is as comfy as the day we got it. The people that had the single beds had to resort to sleeping on the lounge in both cabins. |The cabins are expensive and at the price we paid I’d expect at least a comfy bed.

The park is well laid out with both permanent and holiday caravans and lodges. The grounds are well maintained. When we were there, both BBQs available for guests were not working and we were told by another camper that they had been out of action for some time. Staff were friendly and the rates were very reasonable.

Rooms were a bit spotty here and there but overall it was a pretty enjoyable experience. staff was lovely and appreciated our concerns. the surfari tents have a perfect view of the beach and a short walk to them. Overall, a pretty nice place to stay with family.

Never stayed here, and never will.
Prices absolutely ridiculous.
Kingy South, even worse.

Great place to stay. Second time we have stayed here, this time in a cabin which is well equipped. Unfortunately staying with an infant they do not have any porta cots available but we made it work. Very friendly staff with amazing services on our late check in details. Thank you for having us.

I just stayed in one of the glam tents, very well set out. Could do with something to cook on, such as a hotplate at something similar. Loved going to sleep listening to the waves crashing, noisy but great.

My only whinge is the steps leading in, for someone who has a fused ankle I found a little hard to manoeuvre and I am in my seventies there is nothing to hold on to.

Beautiful place

Typical noisey caravan park. Great location, good grassed/concrete sites. Right on the beach although only patrolled by SLSC Xmas & Easter holidays. Like most parks it is noisey the park caretaker drives a noisey petrol ride on mower around causing air & noise pollution, along with petrol blowers & trimmers. The cleaning staff have a petrol buggy as well. It's about time electric machines are legislated to be required in all caravan parks.
Had a great stay here with family & friends. Definitely recommend it & we'll be going back.

Got a powered tent site to set up our Jayco Camper, it had astro turf on the ground which I'd never seen before, it was awesome, saved setting up mats etc and you can peg stuff down through it. Nice on the feet instead of sand or dirt or concrete. Staff were excellent from arrival to departure. Right in front of the beach. Thanks be back again next year.

I rang up and made a booking for April and either I got the date wrong or they did but in the end they stuck by their Ts and Cs and didn't refund any money and couldn't rebook me. The reason for this 1-star isn't because of the mistake or the $150 deposit that they kept it is because the lady on the other end of the phone was so rude and unwilling to hear any other reasoning behind the error made, I'm honestly happy I'm not staying here. I booked somewhere else for the CORRECT dates and Im sure it will be nice.
Excellent! Good location. Beach just in front of your doorstep.

This place is great! Every year my extended family all book sites here to stay for the long weekend. Great facilities, always clean and fresh. The camp kitchen is beautiful and a great place to hang out and eat. Kids can ride around on the concrete paths and keep entertained easily. You are surrounded by beach on one side, and shops with great food on the other. Music at night from the pubs can be a little loud, but they quiet down at around 8-9. I definitely recommend

We arrived at kingscliff  for a sisters time away all I can say what a beautiful  clean very welcoming  place to stay with beach right at our door step places to eat within walking distance  great place to stay enjoy our time will be comming again thankyou for having us
We love this place. So popular it can be hard to get in but we just book random weekends and love it! Always immaculately kept, friendly and close to everything.

What a fantastic park! It's one of the cleanest parks we've stayed at so far. We were able to get an ensuite site with our van, which made life easier with our 18 month old. The camp kitchen was fantastic and always kept clean. The sites were very generous with their size, which made backing the van in very easy. Staff were very welcoming and polite, as were the other guests. Will definitely be back in the future!
Located right on the beach straight opposite the shops
The only place to stay on the Tweed. Perfect location. Wonderful staff. 😊😊 Every Christmas holiday for the last 51 yrs (except renovation time)

First time visitors to this park and have to say Kingscliff Beach Caravan Park is outstanding.|We had a powered ensuite caravan site and our private ammenities were spotless|Roomy sites and plenty of other powered sites with a view of the ocean further down the park.Lovely Kingscliff Bowling club club next door within walking distance and family friendly serving great meals. Also food vans visit the park weekly and Reception keep you updated with activities and happenings in and around the park. A short stroll to cafes and shops with a coded gate access from the park into town make this an ideal spot if you are looking to relax along the |coast. No pets at this park, great camp kitchen and a peaceful place to just take in the surroundings. A pathway that follows the coastline just outside the park with a ramp and gate access. Plenty of paved walkways and very flat to get around for mobility.|Very safe park with boom gates for entry and always friendly staff moving about the park.|Suggest you book early as this is a very popular spot.|Will be returning in 2024.

The park is in a great location with beach views and direct access to the East and direct access to the shops, cafes and restaurants on the west.

Well maintained and super clean park.

If you’re looking for a beachside location an easy drive to the Gold Coast or Northern rivers then this is it.
This has been an outstanding experience.  Our first time in our caravan,  the park is fantastic on all levels. Plenty of room to get your van on site and the amenities are clean and modern

Very easy place for a break
Right on the ocean, walking distance to shops, hotel, any thing you would need.
Strongly recommend it.
High Tea was a great way to finish one of the national park walks. It is important to book
The cabin we are in has a fantastic view, and you feel like you are on the beach
Great location,  double concrete slabs for caravans, wide road in park so easy to park, amenities new and clean, will be back again soon,
Beautiful place family friendly...
We stayed for a night on a powered site. Closest spot to the beach. Hot shower, nice and modern. Friendly customer service. Recommended.
Everything one could need. Very clean and quiet
Beautifully presented park right on the beach, competitive rates, plenty of space, nice and clean and easy to get into with the van, nice wide plots,  had a very good time , recommend to everyone 😀
We love this place - we stay in the on site tents, they are clean and the bed is super comfy. Staff are great and the location, about a ten minute walk from Kingscliff is ideal.
We stayed in a cabin for the weekend and it was fantastic value! Two bedrooms, clean, comfy beds, coffee machine!, plus right on the beach. Couldn’t ask for more!

This park is small quiet with no pets. It was beautifully maintained. The facilities are terrific. The staff very friendly and helpful. We had a good sized powered site about 25m from the beach. The ensuite was larger than usual, in great condition and with ample hot water. We would highly recommend Kingscliff North caravan park.

Absolute gem of a park. Beautifully kept grounds and amenities.|Friendliest managers and staff that we have come across in our travels.|Whales sighted from our site and beach less than 20 steps away.|Highly recommend staying here and we will definitely be back.
Great place, clean, beachfront and friendly service.
What a great place to spend a weekend...everything was perfect from our arrival to our departure....nothing to fault.
Close to beach,  modern comfortable cabins, caravan and tent sites
We stayed in a cabin for the weekend, spectacular location, staff were friendly and helpful, amenities were well kept and clean.

Just the nicest, most helpful staff.  😁  Very clean amenities and grounds.  Well laid out areas for caravans, camping or Safari Tents. We stayed in Tent #4.  Sublime location,  view of the ocean, beach just through the gate.  Lovely deck, great allocated carpark nect to the tent.
Inside the tent, good amenities fridge, microwave, toaster, kettle, crockery  cutlery, sink, tea, coffee, sugar sachets etc.  Lovely sheets, fluffy towels...NOTE no blanket,  just a bed cover.  Bed is comfortable a softer style mattress, great pillows.  Sofa not very comfortable, sadly quite hard to relax on.
The inside is showing a bit of wear and tear, with sections of the canvas frayed, torn, stained and some zippers separated from the canvas.  Scuff marks on cupboards and timber floors.  A few stains in sofa.
Shower great, good press and hot water.  The tent flap between living area and ensure is pretty annoying to roll up and down or push through when left down.
We love it though and will be back and try and get the couples Safari Tent!
Parked up here last year and again this year. Quiet spot, impeccably maintained, great staff. On the beach (no views) the glamping tents have ocean views. A short walk to town.
